text: Conservation of Momentum for a CV with Rectilinear Acceleration The global momentum conservation for a stationary 1) \( \rho \vec{u} \) is the momentum (per unit volume) of fluid inside \( V \). According to an advanced analysis, the fluid velocity along the streamline from \( A \) to \( B \) is given by: \[ \stackrel{\rightharpoonup}{u}=\underbrace{V_{0}\left(1+\frac{R^{3}}{x^{3}}\right)}_{V_{x}} \hat{\imath} \] where \( V_{0} \) is the velocity far upstream and \( R \) is the radius of the sphere. Find: An expression for the acceleration along the streamline \( \mathrm{A}-\mathrm{B} \) (using the Substantial Derivative) and the acceleration and velocity at Point \( B=(-R, 0) \). Note: You should not convert into cylindrical or polar coordinates. \[ \frac{D \vec{u}}{D t}=\frac{\partial \vec{u}}{\partial t}+(\vec{u} \cdot \nabla) \vec{u}=\frac{\partial \vec{y}}{\partial t}+u_{x} \frac{\partial \vec{u}}{\partial x}+u_{0}\left(\frac{\partial \vec{u}}{\partial y}+u_{z} / \frac{\partial \vec{u}}{\partial z}=u_{x} \frac{\partial \vec{u}}{\partial x}\right. \] But \( u_{x}=v_{0}\left(1+\frac{R^{3}}{x^{3}}\right)^{\text {(stated) }} \) and \( \frac{\partial \vec{u}}{\partial x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(v_{0}\left(1+R^{3} x^{-3}\right) R\right)=v_{0} R^{3}\left(-3 x^{-4}\right) \hat{\imath} \vec{a}=V_{0}\left(1+\frac{R^{3}}{x^{3}}\right) \cdot V_{0} R^{3}\left(-3 x^{-4}\right) \hat{\imath} \) Accelertion unly in \( x \)-direction: At \( (x, y)=(-R, O) \) \[ \vec{a}=V_{0}(\underbrace{\left(1+\frac{R^{3}}{-R^{3}}\right)}_{1-1=0} \cdot V_{0} R^{3}\left(-3 R^{4}\right) \hat{\imath}=0 \quad \vec{u}=V_{0}\left(1+\frac{R^{3}}{-R^{3}}\right)=0 \]

text: Given a velocity field, how can we find streamlines? Since \( \overrightarrow{d S} \) is parallel to \( \vec{u} \) : \[ \begin{array}{c} \overrightarrow{d S} \times \vec{u}=0 . \\ \overrightarrow{d S} \times \vec{u}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \vec{i} & \vec{j} & \vec{k} \\ d x & d y & d z \\ u & v & w \end{array}\right|=\begin{array}{c} (w d y-v d z) \hat{c} \\ -(w d x-u d z) \hat{s} \\ +(v d x-u d y) \hat{m}=0 \\ \text { onlyme if ecch } \\ \text { tem=0 } \end{array} \end{array} \] Finally, we get the following differential equation for streamline: \[ \frac{d x}{u}=\frac{d y}{v}=\frac{d z}{w} \] 2-84
